tldr: Bernard, both of your live ads are running the exact same headline, body, and CTA — it's effectively one creative spending twice. Expect CPM to climb sharply within 7-10 days as frequency stacks against the same audience pocket.

benchmark.context: Fashion ecom brands at your stage typically rotate 6-10 distinct creative angles in-market at once. With two identical 'Your New Favorite Outfit Starts Here' variants live, you're well below category median for creative variety.

ads.headline: Your New Favorite Outfit Starts Here
ads.body: Shop the latest must-have looks from LA ✨ Get 15% OFF your first order — limited time.
drivers: Headline 'Your New Favorite Outfit Starts Here' is duplicated verbatim across both live ads — Meta's algorithm reads this as one creative for frequency capping
Body copy with the LA sparkle emoji and 15% off line is identical to the second variant, so you're not actually testing anything
Generic 'must-have looks from LA' hook gives no product, no silhouette, no reason — fatigue accelerates fast on vague openers

ads.headline: Your New Favorite Outfit Starts Here
ads.body: Shop the latest must-have looks from LA ✨ Get 15% OFF your first order — limited time.
drivers: Carbon copy of the other live variant — same headline, same body, same CTA, same homepage landing
Both ads point to the bare homepage instead of a collection or hero SKU, so even cold traffic that converts has no clear path
'Shop now' CTA paired with a generic homepage drop is the highest-friction combo for first-time buyers in fashion

hero_concept.hook: The babydoll cami doing all the heavy lifting this spring.
hero_concept.headline: Sheer. Soft. Sold Out Twice.
hero_concept.primary_text:
Tic Toc started in LA with one rule: the pieces women actually reach for first should feel as good as they look.

The ROSA V-Neck Lace Trim Babydoll Cami is that piece — sheer where it counts, lace trim that doesn't itch, cut to layer under a blazer or wear with denim and nothing else.

15% off your first order. Ships from LA in 48 hours.
hero_concept.visual_direction: The ROSA babydoll cami styled on a soft, sunlit set with warm LA-afternoon light and a clean editorial backdrop.
hero_concept.fills_gap: Right now both live ads send cold traffic to the homepage with the same generic 'must-have looks from LA' line and no actual product in frame. Leading with a specific hero SKU and a tactile copy angle gives the algorithm a fresh creative signal and gives shoppers a reason to click.
hero_concept.image_prompt: A photorealistic editorial product photograph featuring the product from the reference image, styled in a softly lit California bedroom corner with linen sheets, a vintage wooden chair, and a sunlit window casting golden afternoon light, with warm directional sunlight and gentle shadows that highlight the lace trim and sheer fabric texture. Modern, fashion-forward, feminine aesthetic with a creamy neutral palette — think LA editorial meets cozy minimalism. Square 1:1 composition with breathing room top and bottom for ad text overlay. Maintain the exact appearance, color, and details of the product from the reference image. Premium commerce photography quality. NEGATIVE: no text, no logos, no watermarks, no UI elements, no Facebook interface, no buttons, no faces, no collage, no borders, no duplicate products.
hero_concept.reference_title: ROSA V Neck Lace Trim Sheer Babydoll Cami Top

next_step.urgency: Both live ads hit fatigue within 7-8 days — window to swap is now.
next_step.headline: Let's map your next 6 creative angles
next_step.body: Bernard, 30 minutes and we'll plot out the hero SKU rotations, hook variants, and landing pages that should replace the duplicate homepage ads. You'll leave the call with a shoot list and a launch order.

method_note: Signals were scored by walking through every live Tic Toc ad in Meta Ad Library; fatigue projections assume current spend level and audience size.
